channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Revealed: 33,000 patients had data stolen in trust cyber attack
item.description: A hospital provider has admitted that confidential patient information relating to almost 33,000 of its patients was stolen and shared on the dark web, two years after the cyberattack took place.

item.title: First CEO role for former parliamentary candidate
item.description: A doctor who has previously run as a Labour parliamentary candidate has been appointed chief executive of Dartford and Gravesham Trust.

item.title: Substandard dialysis did not kill patients, says trust
item.description: A decision to provide substandard dialysis treatment due to “exceptional” capacity pressure was not responsible for high mortality discovered among the service’s patients, a trust has claimed.

item.title: ICB issues tougher spending rules for local trusts
item.description: Several trusts in the South West are being asked to restrict all non-essential spending due to their “challenging financial position”.

item.title: CQC slashes value of failed IT project
item.description: The Care Quality Commission has written off millions of pounds from the value of the failed IT system that was central to recent problems with its inspection regime.

item.title: Lab fault sees hundreds fast-tracked for unnecessary cancer tests
item.description: More than 1,300 patients were referred for urgent bowel cancer investigations they may not have needed after a calibration error at a shared NHS pathology service.

item.title: Troubleshooter joins trust as interim CEO
item.description: A mental health trust has appointed an interim chief with a reputation for taking on challenged organisations while it begins a search for its next permanent leader.

item.title: Retail exec to lead NHS digital trust
item.description: A senior retail sector executive has been appointed the first chair of NHS Online, the digital-only trust which was formally launched today.

item.title: Top trust ‘struggling’ with tariff reform
item.description: A top-performing trust’s CEO has said it is “struggling” with the impact of NHS England’s financial reforms – which it argues are “disproportionately” affecting organisations providing specialist care.

item.title: The 50 most influential people for NHS tech in 2026: The full list
item.description: Top 10 1. Lawrence Tallon, CEO, Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency Mr Tallon is leading the MHRA into a new and challenging era. The regulator had fallen behind on overseeing artificial intelligence and other technology, and the former Guy’s and St Thomas’ Foundation Trust deputy chief ...

item.title: Regulator boss named top NHS tech leader
item.description: HSJ’s first top 50 list of tech influencers shows how diverse and diffuse power is in this sector.

item.title: Hundreds of overseas NHSE staff face losing job with no compensation
item.description: Hundreds of NHS England staff face losing their jobs without redundancy pay because their nationality bars them from working for the Department of Health and Social Care, HSJ understands.

item.title: Health Bill proposal an ‘attack on patient safety’
item.description: Scrapping the legal guarantee that a nurse sits on every foundation trust board is a “brazen attack on patient safety”, the Royal College of Nursing has warned.

item.title: Hospital accused of delaying ambulances to eliminate corridor care
item.description: A hospital trust has been accused of delaying the offloading of ambulance patients so it can maintain zero “corridor care”.

item.title: Revealed: ICBs facing clash between PCNs and neighbourhood health
item.description: Dozens of primary care networks in some areas may need to be reorganised to take on neighbourhood contracts, because they do not cover a coherent geographic area.

item.title: Trusts begin EPR rollouts after NHSE-imposed delays
item.description: Three trusts have begun rolling out their electronic patient record systems months later than planned after NHS England forced them to delay.

item.title: When is a cover-up not a cover-up?
item.description: The answer to the question posed in our headline is apparently: “When the decision to keep something quiet is based on clinical advice.”

item.title: ‘Inspirational’ chair dies after short illness
item.description: The chair of a mental health trust has died following a short illness, with its CEO paying tribute to the “inspirational leader”.

item.title: New chief for troubled hospital
item.description: The deputy chief executive of two south coast trusts has been appointed to one of the toughest jobs in the NHS.

item.title: Resident doctors to strike during by-election
item.description: Resident doctors will strike for four days next month – with the threat of more action in July – after talks with the new health and social care secretary failed to produce a breakthrough.
